# nomad_acl_token (Ephemeral Resource)

Reads a Nomad ACL token when the token secret is needed by Terraform but should not be stored in state.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "nomad_acl_token" "build" {
  type     = "client"
  policies = ["dev"]
}

ephemeral "nomad_acl_token" "build" {
  accessor_id = nomad_acl_token.build.accessor_id
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `accessor_id` (String) Nomad-generated ID for this token.

### Read-Only

- `create_time` (String) The timestamp the token was created.
- `expiration_time` (String) The point after which a token is considered expired and eligible for destruction.
- `expiration_ttl` (String) Provides a TTL for the token in the form of a time duration such as "5m" or "1h".
- `global` (Boolean) Whether the token should be replicated to all regions or not.
- `name` (String) Human-readable name for this token.
- `policies` (Set of String) List of policy names associated with this token.
- `roles` (Attributes Set) The roles that are applied to the token. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--roles))
- `secret_id` (String, Sensitive) The value that grants access to Nomad.
- `type` (String) The type of the token.

<a id="nestedatt--roles"></a>
### Nested Schema for `roles`

Read-Only:

- `id` (String) The ID of the ACL role.
- `name` (String) The name of the ACL role.
